question: How do styles affect lane breakdown?
content:
High rev bowlers
Put more rotation and more friction into the lane.
Their ball digs earlier and stronger.
Result: They remove oil faster, especially in the midlane and inside part of the lane. This creates earlier hook zones and forces moves left sooner.
Low rev bowlers
Create less friction and therefore don’t strip oil as quickly.
But they usually play more outside, closer to the friction track.
Result: They open up the outside part of the lane, creating a defined friction spot that high rev players can later “bounce off” when the inside starts to go.
The Simple Takeaway
High revs = faster breakdown, mostly inside.
Low revs = slower breakdown, but they shape the outside track.
Together, they create the classic transition: outside burns first → inside follows → everyone moves left.
